chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الدُّعَاءِ عِنْدَ النَّوْمِ وَ الِانْتِبَاهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – The supplication at sleep time and when waking up

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/314)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/314)
text: تَقُولُ إِذَا أَرَدْتَ النَّوْمَ اللَّهُمَّ إِنْ أَمْسَكْتَ نَفْسِي فَارْحَمْهَا وَ إِنْ أَرْسَلْتَهَا فَاحْفَظْهَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his fataher, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of his companions, raising it,

‘He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘You should be saying whenever you intend to sleep, ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! If You<sup>azwj</sup> are to Withhold my soul so be Merciful to it, and if You<sup>azwj</sup> are to Send it (back to me), so Protect it’’.
en.sarwar: 14. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from certain individuals of his people in a marfu ’ manner from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “When you are about to sleep say, ‘O Lord, if You holdback my soul from returning to my body, have mercy on it and if You will allow it to return, please protect it. ’”
